summary: SCH4U Unit 2 problems name organic molecules with IUPAC rules and recognize isomers and functional groups. Naming pins down the longest carbon chain, the branches and where they attach, and any functional group that defines the family.
formulas: structural isomers: same formula, different connectivity
method: Find the longest continuous carbon chain — that sets the root name (meth, eth, prop, but, …).
Identify the functional group to choose the suffix (-ane, -ene, -ol, -oic acid, …).
Number the chain from the end nearest the first point of difference.
Name and number each branch (substituent) and list them alphabetically.
Combine substituents + root + suffix into the full IUPAC name.

example.title: Name the isomers of C₄H₁₀
example.find: the two structural isomers and their names
steps: C₄H₁₀ fits CₙH₂ₙ₊₂, so it is a saturated alkane.
A straight four-carbon chain is butane.
Branching the chain gives a three-carbon chain with a methyl group on the middle carbon.
